subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, CBTT, Freedom House, RSF, InSight Crime, Migration Policy Institute) + international and Trinidadian press.

The wealthiest-per-capita Caribbean economy on oil/gas, cushioned by a ~US$6.4bn Heritage & Stabilisation Fund, but facing structural gas decline, a stalled diversification, chronic FX shortages draining reserves, and severe violent crime.

Standout drags: declining gas output and stalled diversification, a decades-low ~5.4-months FX reserve cover, record-high gang violence (a late-2024 state of emergency), and an acutely volatile Venezuela border.

economic.notes: A wealthy energy economy with low inflation and a sovereign buffer, against structural gas decline and FX rationing.

fiscal_state.notes: Deficit ~5.5% of GDP (FY25) narrowing to ~4.6%, debt ~68% (public-sector ~84%), but a US$6.38bn Heritage & Stabilisation Fund is a sizeable buffer. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The TTD is a de-facto USD-managed rate; defending it drains reserves and produces chronic FX rationing - limited true autonomy. (IMF 2026)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The TTD is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and stable (~2.4% end-2025) - a genuine strength. (CBTT)

banking_stability.notes: Broadly stable and well-capitalised, but heavy bank/insurer exposure to sovereign debt and rising household indebtedness are flagged. (CBTT Financial Stability Report)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Highly concentrated in oil/gas/LNG/petrochemicals with declining gas feedstock; diversification is nascent and slow. (IMF 2026)

institutional.notes: A functioning democracy and usable data, with persistent corruption and journalist-safety concerns.

rule_of_law.notes: A functioning parliamentary democracy and courts, but persistent official corruption and organized-crime penetration weigh. (Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: CSO/CBTT data are usable and IMF-engaged, though the CSO has faced timeliness/capacity criticism. (IMF; CBTT)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Reasonable capacity for the region, but bureaucratic and FX-allocation frictions impede the non-energy economy. (IMF 2026)

press_freedom.notes: Vibrant media, but RSF flags deteriorating journalist safety (no witness-protection, self-censorship); rank fell ~13 to 32nd. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: A stable two-party system under SOE-era civil-liberties strain, with an ethnic cleavage and migrant-crackdown.

political_stability.notes: The UNC's Persad-Bissessar won a decisive majority over the PNM in Apr 2025 - an orderly transfer in a stable two-party system. (Newsday; Atlantic Council)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Free', but repeated states of emergency with warrantless search/arrest powers raise real concerns. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A sharp hardening toward Venezuelan migrants - work permits cut from ~4,275 (2024) to 757 (2025), deportation orders, threatened 'mass deportation'. (Migration Policy Institute)

social_cohesion.notes: An Afro-/Indo-Trinidadian ethnic-political cleavage plus ~180 territorial gangs fuel violence and polarized politics. (InSight Crime)

demographics.notes: A relatively favourable structure, but skilled emigration and energy dependence cloud the long run. (general)

geopolitical.notes: A US-aligned democracy on the front line of US-Venezuela tensions, with OFAC-dependent cross-border gas.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable US-aligned CARICOM democracy that has tacked closer to the US on Venezuela, but the abrupt Venezuela rupture shows regional volatility. (Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: Not a combatant, but on the front line of US-Venezuela Caribbean tensions and arms/migrant spillover, with a Venezuela diplomatic rupture. (Migration Policy Institute)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions on T&T, but its key Dragon gas project depends on OFAC licensing (revoked, then a 6-month license Oct 2025); FX rationing acts like soft controls. (Newsday; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Below the main hurricane belt and relatively developed, against record gang violence.

climate.notes: A higher-income, lower-hurricane-frequency SIDS, but coastal/SLR plus ecosystem stress, with an oil economy limiting transition readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: A public system with broad coverage and higher per-capita spend than peers, with quality/wait-time gaps and a private tier. (general)

infrastructure.notes: Relatively developed (energy, roads, telecoms) but FX shortages and ageing energy assets constrain investment. (IMF 2026)

crime_safety.notes: 2024 was the deadliest year on record (~614-624 homicides); a Dec-2024 SOE drove a ~44% drop in 2025, but violence is structurally high and gang-driven. (InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Lower catastrophe exposure than hurricane-belt peers, an insurable market with industrial/energy-accident tail risk. (general)

summary: The wealthiest-per-capita Caribbean economy, anchored by oil/gas/LNG/petrochemicals and a substantial ~US$6.4bn Heritage & Stabilisation Fund, with low inflation and a stable two-party democracy that delivered an orderly handover to the UNC's Kamla Persad-Bissessar in April 2025. But the structural picture is negative: mature-field gas output keeps declining, diversification has stalled, growth is stuck near 0.8%, and defending the de-facto USD peg has drained reserves to a decades-low ~5.4 months of imports amid chronic FX rationing the IMF wants addressed. Compounding this are a severe gang-violence problem (a record-deadly 2024 and a late-2024 state of emergency) and an acutely volatile Venezuela border - migrant crackdowns, a diplomatic rupture, and OFAC-dependent cross-border gas (Dragon). A rich but resource-trapped state whose fortunes are tightly bound to energy prices and Venezuela.
